preview_text:
The model favours Czech Republic at 54%, with South Africa offered a 17% chance of victory. The draw sits at 29%, reflecting meaningful uncertainty in a fixture the model rates as competitive but tilted toward the favourite.

The spread between the top two outcomes spans roughly 25 percentage points, indicating a clear favourite but one without commanding control of the match. A draw remains plausible at nearly three-in-ten, suggesting the teams are close enough in underlying strength that a stalemate carries real probability. The match is unlikely to be a rout; South Africa's 17% win chance implies it enters as a credible underdog rather than an afterthought.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both lean slightly more bullish on Czech Republic than the model—by 3–4 percentage points—while compressing the draw marginally. These gaps fall within typical variance across betting sources and remain small enough to indicate broad alignment. No meaningful divergence between model and market warrant flagging here.
